item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Orson Welles' 1938 "War of the Worlds" broadcast
item.description: On Oct. 30, 1938, CBS News Radio listeners were sent into a frenzy listening to Orson Welles' reading of H.G. Wells' "War of the Worlds," thinking it was a real broadcast detailing an alien invasion. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns introduces the fictional, now-iconic breaking news bulletin.

item.title: House has spent $338,000 since 2004 to settle harassment claims against lawmakers, Rep. Mace claims
item.description: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina says a subpoena revealed that secret payouts used to settle sexual harassment claims against lawmakers have totaled $338,000 since 2004. Former Democratic Rep. Jackie Speier of California joins "The Takeout" with her reaction.

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court tosses out congressional map that favored Democrats
item.description: The congressional redistricting referendum was passed by Virginia voters last month and would have given Democrats a more favorable map.

item.title: Democrats probe what they call "suspicious circumstances" of some Trump pardons
item.description: Congressional Democrats are investigating whether so-called "pay-to-play" schemes drove some of President Trump's pardons. Democratic Rep. Dave Min of California, one of the three lawmakers who launched the probe, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: U.S. awaiting Iran's response to peace proposal
item.description: The war with Iran started nearly 10 weeks ago and the path toward peace remains unclear. Iran has been reviewing the latest U.S. proposal to end the conflict. CBS News politics reporter Kathryn Watson has more.

item.title: Hantavirus cruise ship expected to arrive in Canary Islands, sparks local protests
item.description: U.S. health officials say they are sending a plane to bring home 19 Americans who are on board the Dutch cruise ship at the Center of the hantavirus outbreak. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio reports and Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire still intact after exchanging attacks with Iran
item.description: President Trump insists the ceasefire with Iran is still in effect despite both sides admitting to strikes in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News contributor Ret. Lt. Col. Roger Carstens joins "The Takeout" with analysis.

item.title: U.S. fires on 2 more tankers as Washington expects Iran response on deal
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. was expecting a response from Iran on a peace proposal Friday, adding, "we'll see what the response entails" and that he hopes it's "a serious offer."

item.title: Comparing hantavirus and COVID-19
item.description: The cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak will dock in the Canary Islands, off the coast of Spain, at some point on Sunday. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins "The Takeout" to help separate fact from fear.

item.title: Fed unlikely to cut interest rates until 2027, Bank of America says
item.description: A "hawkish" turn at the Fed and stubbornly high inflation could delay interest rate cuts, according to Bank of America economists.

item.title: U.S. plans evacuation for Americans on cruise ship in hantavirus outbreak
item.description: The MV Hondius is currently traveling to the Canary Islands, where the 147 people on board will be methodically off-boarded and flown home.

item.title: "The Age of Disclosure" director on released UFO files: "We're at a major turning point"
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released and declassified numerous files on UFOs, including eyewitness testimony, photos and reports. Government knowledge of non-human intelligent life was the subject of the documentary "The Age of Disclosure," released in February. Its director and producer, Dan Farah, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Republicans see political wins ahead of midterm elections
item.description: Virginia's Supreme Court tossed out a new voter-passed Congressional map that favored Democrats on Friday. CBS News' Fin Gómez breaks it all down.

item.title: Senior SETI Institute astronomer reacts to new Pentagon UFO files
item.description: The Pentagon is releasing and declassifying files on UFOs. Seth Shostak, senior astronomer at the SETI Institute, joins CBS News with analysis.

item.title: ABC accuses the FCC of violating free speech rights over "The View"
item.description: ABC filed a petition with the FCC claiming that the agency's scrutiny of "The View" threatens to "chill critical protected speech."

item.title: Here's how AI can help with retirement planning, and where it struggles
item.description: As more people turn to chatbots for financial advice, experts say AI offers both pros and cons for retirement planning. Here's what to know.

item.title: Trump announces prisoner swap in 3-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said the agreement will swap 1,000 prisoners from each country after Russia and Ukraine announced separate short-term ceasefire plans.

item.title: State Department reviewing all Mexican consulates in U.S. as tensions grow
item.description: The State Department is initiating a review of all 53 Mexican consulates in the United States, a U.S. official said, a move that could lead Secretary of State Marco Rubio to consider ordering the closure of some posts.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 10)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Extended interview: Lizzo
item.description: Lizzo sits down with "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King to discuss her third album, "Bitch," and how it reflects her current state in life, her ongoing legal battle, weight loss journey, embracing her body and more.

item.title: What the Trump administration's latest tariff blow means for businesses
item.description: A trade court's ruling this week against a 10% U.S. tariff is narrow in scope, offering limited relief to importers. Here's what to know.

item.title: What's the required minimum distribution from a $300,000 retirement account?
item.description: Have $300,000 saved in a retirement account? Here are the required minimum distributions you'll be expected to take.

item.title: Pentagon begins releasing UFO files: "It's time the American people see"
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a statement that the documents "have long fueled justified speculation — and it's time the American people see it for themselves."

item.title: Watch videos of possible UFOs from first batch of files released by Pentagon
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday began releasing more unclassified files on UFOs and UAPs, including more than two dozen videos. Here are all the videos released in the first batch of files.

item.title: $100,000 CD vs. $100,000 high-yield savings account vs. $100,000 money market account: Here's which will earn more interest now
item.description: All three savings account types can offer a big return on your $100,000 deposit. Here's which one will earn the most.

item.title: Trump ally Farage says elections mark "historic shift in British politics"
item.description: One U.K. lawmaker says big election losses for the ruling Labour Party and its main rivals show the de-facto two-party system is "not just dying, it is dead."

item.title: What are today's mortgage interest rates: May 8, 2026?
item.description: Want to buy a home or refinance your current one? Here are the mortgage interest rates you'll need to know first.

item.title: AI is as a top cause of layoffs, accounting for 26% of April's job cuts
item.description: New data shows artificial intelligence is the most cited reason for layoffs, even as economists debate whether it is truly displacing workers.

item.title: Trump pardon recipients face congressional probe over "pay-to-play" questions
item.description: Congressional Democrats are investigating clemency recipients who may have obtained favorable treatment from Trump or his advisers "through intermediaries, financial contributions, or other forms of influence."

item.title: Mom urges ban on water beads after daughter's injury: "It breaks my heart"
item.description: Water beads may look harmless and fun, but the authors of a new report​ — including a mom whose daughter was seriously injured by them — are calling for a ban​ on their sale.
